_comment: v2 evals for the orchestrator + per-stage-rubric architecture. ZENW (Zenwave Systems) is a fictional company whose fixtures were generated by evals/files/make_zenw_fixtures.py, so cases 1, 3, 4, and 6 are hermetic (no EDGAR, no web). Case 2 is the live end-to-end case and needs network access. Dates inside prompts are pinned so the evals stay deterministic. See evals/README.md for how to run.

evals.name: stage3-entry-gate-b-ambiguous-only
evals.prompt: Assume today is 2026-06-10. Setup: copy the fixture workbook ZENW_source_workbook.xlsx (provided as an input file) to coverage/ZENW/ZENW_Integrated_Financials.xlsx in your working directory — that is the assembled Stage-2 output. I want to analyze Zenwave Systems, Inc. (ZENW). The workbook already has Income Statement, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow Statement tabs: FY2021–FY2025, USD millions, headers in row 6 (fiscal year-end dates), data from row 7. Start from Stage 3 — build the condensed financials and the DuPont analysis. Pause and let me review the balance-sheet classifications before you build the aggregations. So the run can complete: when you present the classification review, take these as my answers — operating lease ROU assets and both lease liabilities = operating; deferred tax assets and liabilities = operating; pension obligations = operating long-term liability; short-term investments = financial asset; equity-method investments = operating long-term asset.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects the existing workbook (skips Stages 1–2), runs Stage 3 per references/stage3_analyst.md, pauses at Gate B showing ONLY the ambiguous classifications with their analytical implications, applies the user's answers, then builds the formula-driven Condensed Financials and ALT DuPont tabs, verifies NOA = Total Capital and the DuPont reconciliation, persists scripts/build_condensed.py, and reports headline RNOA/ROE.
expectations: The run paused for a classification review (Gate B) before building the condensed balance-sheet aggregations.

The Gate B review presented ONLY the ambiguous items — operating lease ROU assets / lease liabilities, deferred income tax assets and liabilities, pension obligations, short-term investments, and equity-method investments — each with its default, the alternative, and the analytical implication (effect on NOA / RNOA / FLEV).
Unambiguous lines (cash, accounts receivable, inventories, accounts payable, long-term debt) were auto-classified and NOT presented for review at Gate B.
The Condensed Financials tab computes NOPAT as Net Income + Net Interest After Tax, with the interest lines taken from the Income Statement tab by formula (not hardcoded, not silently zeroed).

The condensed balance-sheet aggregates (Operating WC Assets, Operating WC Liabilities, NOWC, Operating LT Assets/Liabilities, NOA, Financial Assets/Liabilities, Net Debt, Total Capital) are driven by the classification cells (SUMIF over a classification column, or a documented fallback), and the classification column has Data Validation dropdowns with the six categories.
Every cell in the new tabs links back to the source tabs or other condensed cells by Excel formula — no hardcoded financial values appear outside the IS/BS/CF source tabs.

The NOA = Total Capital check passes for all five years (FY2021–FY2025) and the result is reported. With the user's classifications the fixture is exactly consistent, so any failure is a build bug, not a data problem.

The ALT DuPont tab uses the rubric's exact row labels (Sales Growth, NOPAT Margin, Avg NOA, Asset Turnover, RNOA, Avg Net Debt, After-tax CoD, Spread, Avg Equity, FLEV, Leverage Gain, ROE (decomposed), Actual ROE, Check), shows n/a for the oldest year, and the decomposed ROE reconciles with actual ROE each year.
Because ZENW has negative Net Debt every year, the DuPont tab (or the report) includes the note that FLEV is negative and leverage REDUCES ROE relative to RNOA.
The build code was persisted to coverage/ZENW/scripts/build_condensed.py.
The final message reports the headline RNOA and ROE by year (FY2025 RNOA is roughly 49% on the fixture data).

evals.name: googl-full-pipeline-all-gates
evals.prompt: Run the full BAV pipeline for Alphabet (GOOGL) in a fresh working directory (assume no coverage/ exists yet — do not reuse any prior GOOGL artifacts you may find elsewhere). I don't have any files — just the ticker. Do the strategy research, pull the SEC financial data, build the condensed model, and run the multi-scenario valuation. Pause at each review gate so I can check your work; at each gate, state what you are asking, then proceed with your recommended answer so the run can complete, and record that you did so.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrated 4-stage run with subagents per stage rubric: Stage 1 strategy (web research) then GATE A on scenario assumptions/probabilities/KPI watchlist; Stage 2 EDGAR assembly with NO gate (restatements auto-resolved newest-filing-wins, logged in extraction_log.md); Stage 3 then GATE B on ambiguous classifications only; Stage 4 producing assumption_map.md before any model code, then GATE C terminal-return calibration; then the coverage-vault finish sequence (results + history snapshot, valuation_log row, dossier, journal, _universe, three persisted scripts).
expectations: The four stages ran strictly in order as separate subagent invocations (or, if the Agent tool was unavailable, the orchestrator explicitly said so before inlining the stage rubrics).

GATE A was presented after Stage 1 with: a scenario differentiation matrix, the per-scenario assumption summary (Y1/terminal growth and margin, betas), probability weights, and a KPI watchlist with checkable thresholds; figures that are estimates rather than sourced were flagged.
No review gate ran after Stage 2: restatement conflicts were auto-resolved newest-filing-wins and logged in extraction_log.md rather than asked about.
GATE B presented only ambiguous balance-sheet classifications (items flagged with a warning marker), not the full classification table.

Stage 4 produced coverage/GOOGL/assumption_map.md BEFORE building model tabs, tracing every model input (growth, margin, NOWC/NOLA ratios, betas, terminal targets) to a named strategy-report element, including the segment-blend reconciliation within ±0.5pp.

GATE C presented IVPS per scenario plus probability-weighted vs. current price, and terminal RNOA with its multiple of cost of equity per scenario, each given a calibration verdict against the bands Bear 1.0–1.5x, Base 1.5–2.5x, Bull 2.5–3.5x CoE.
strategy_report.md contains NO model results — no IVPS values and no terminal RNOA figures appear anywhere in it; results appear in memo.md (and the dossier) instead.

assumptions.json conforms to canonical schema v2 (schemaVersion 2, scenarios keyed Bear/Base/Bull, four 10-element vectors per scenario as decimals, provenance per vector, segments that blend to the top-line growth vector within ±0.5pp, kpiWatchlist rows) and its results block was filled only after Gate C.

After Gate C acceptance the finish sequence ran: assumptions.json snapshotted into history/, one valuation_log.csv row with trigger initial_build, dossier.md written with complete YAML frontmatter (weighted_ivps, price + date, gap, probabilities, last_update, next_catalyst, status), a dated journal.md entry recording the gate decisions, and coverage/_universe.md updated.
coverage/GOOGL/scripts/ contains the three build scripts (build_statements.py, build_condensed.py, build_model.py).
Only the IS/BS/CF source tabs contain hardcoded data; Condensed, DuPont, Model, and Scenario_Summary tabs are formula-driven, and column headers on source tabs are real date cells, not strings.
The final chat message states weighted IVPS vs. price in one sentence, where the files are, and points to bav-update as the maintenance path.

evals.name: stage4-assumption-map-and-gate-c
evals.prompt: Assume today is 2026-06-10. Setup: copy these fixtures into your working directory — ZENW_analysis_workbook.xlsx to coverage/ZENW/ZENW_Integrated_Financials.xlsx, ZENW_strategy_report.md to coverage/ZENW/strategy_report.md, and ZENW_assumptions.json to coverage/ZENW/assumptions.json. Stages 1–3 of the BAV pipeline are already done for Zenwave Systems (ZENW): the workbook has source tabs plus Condensed Financials and ALT DuPont (values cached), the strategy report is approved, and assumptions.json is the confirmed post-Gate-A/B draft with results: null. Skip straight to Stage 4 — build the multi-scenario residual income model and take it through the final review. At the final gate, present the package, then proceed with your recommendation so the run can complete.
evals.expected_output: Stage 4 per references/stage4_modeler.md: assumption_map.md as Step 0 (with segment reconciliation and consistency checks), then Model_Bear/Base/Bull tabs + Scenario_Summary built by formula, the Gate C calibration package (all three scenarios land inside their target bands on this fixture), memo.md with the results, results written to assumptions.json with a history snapshot, valuation log row, dossier and universe updated.
expectations: coverage/ZENW/assumption_map.md was produced BEFORE any model-building code ran, as a table tracing every model input to a verbatim or cited strategy-report element (Bear/Bull hypotheses, Model Inputs Summary, watchlist rows, moat assessment).

The assumption map includes the segment reconciliation for Y1–3: share-weighted segment growth vs. the top-line growthVector, shown per year, all within ±0.5pp (the fixture reconciles by construction).

Internal-consistency checks were performed and reported: Bull margins never below Base, Bear growth never above Base, beta ordering Bear >= Base >= Bull, identical diluted shares across scenarios, terminal growth <= 4% and below cost of equity in every scenario.
Anchor consistency was checked: assumptions.json anchorRevenue (11,400) matches the workbook's FY2025 revenue.

Model_Bear, Model_Base, and Model_Bull tabs plus Scenario_Summary were added to the workbook; cost of equity is computed by formula from beta/ERP/risk-free (never hardcoded), and the historical columns link to the source/Condensed tabs by formula.

Each model tab carries scenario-specific Professor's Notes naming that scenario's mechanisms (e.g. white-box substitution for Bear, AI-telemetry attach for Bull) — not one templated note reused three times.

The Gate C package presented: IVPS per scenario and probability-weighted vs. the $84.00 price, terminal RNOA and ROE per scenario, and the RNOA/CoE multiple per scenario with calibration verdicts against Bear 1.0–1.5x, Base 1.5–2.5x, Bull 2.5–3.5x.

On this fixture all three scenarios calibrate inside their bands (approximately 1.25x / 2.0x / 3.0x), no scenario exceeds 5x CoE, and the run proceeded WITHOUT a recalibration bounce; the weighted IVPS lands below the $84.00 price (the fixture's model value is in the rough vicinity of $50).

memo.md was written with the valuation table, what-the-market-must-believe framing, the calibration table, key sensitivities, and the watchlist items most likely to move the valuation — and the strategy report was NOT modified to contain any of these results.
results (per-scenario ivps + terminalRNOA, weightedIVPS, gapVsPricePct, asOf) were written into assumptions.json, with the prior assumptions.json snapshotted to history/ before the write.
valuation_log.csv gained one row and dossier.md frontmatter carries the new weighted_ivps, price with date, gap, and status; coverage/_universe.md has a ZENW row.
scripts/build_model.py was persisted under coverage/ZENW/scripts/.

evals.name: gate-c-5x-coe-bounce
evals.prompt: Assume today is 2026-06-10. Setup: copy these fixtures into your working directory — ZENW_analysis_workbook.xlsx to coverage/ZENW/ZENW_Integrated_Financials.xlsx, ZENW_strategy_report.md to coverage/ZENW/strategy_report.md, and ZENW_assumptions.json to coverage/ZENW/assumptions.json. Stages 1–3 are done for Zenwave Systems (ZENW). I want the model built MY way: I think the asset-intensity ramps are far too conservative, so hold NOWC/Sales flat at 0.01 and NOLA/Sales flat at 0.345 (the FY2025 actuals) for all ten years in ALL three scenarios, and set the Bull margin vector to a flat 20%. Keep everything else from assumptions.json. Run Stage 4 with those overrides and give me the final valuation. If you need my sign-off mid-run, state what you would ask me, then proceed with your recommended resolution and document it.
evals.expected_output: The Modeler builds with the overrides, but Gate C catches that flat asset intensity extrapolates current ~49% RNOA forever: Bull terminal RNOA comes out near 56% (~6x its 9.45% CoE), over the 5x CoE limit. Per the Gate C rule the skill does NOT present that as final — it sends the model back to Stage 4 to raise asset-intensity ramps and/or compress margins, re-gates, and presents a calibrated final, documenting the rejected override and why.
expectations: The run computed terminal RNOA under the user's overrides and identified that at least the Bull scenario exceeds 5x its cost of equity (flat 0.355 NOA/Sales with a 20% margin gives terminal RNOA around 56% against a 9.45% CoE — roughly 6x).

The over-5x model was NOT presented as the final valuation: the skill invoked the Gate C bounce rule — send it back to Stage 4 with instructions to raise asset-intensity vectors and/or compress margins — rather than accepting the user's numbers silently.

The pushback explained WHY in residual-income terms: flat NOWC/NOLA ramps extrapolate current extraordinary returns (~49% RNOA) into perpetuity, which contradicts mean reversion / competitive equilibrium (terminal RNOA must reflect a defensible competitive position).

Calibration verdicts referenced the per-scenario bands (Bear 1.0–1.5x, Base 1.5–2.5x, Bull 2.5–3.5x CoE), noting Bear and Base also sit far above their bands under the overrides even though only Bull crosses the hard 5x limit.
A recalibrated model was then produced and re-gated, with every scenario's terminal RNOA at or below 5x CoE (and brought toward the target bands), and THIS version was presented as final.

The user's requested override and its rejection were documented in the audit trail (journal entry and/or memo), including what was changed in the recalibration and the resulting terminal RNOA multiples.
The final artifacts are consistent with the recalibrated model, not the broken one: assumptions.json results, memo.md, valuation_log.csv, and dossier.md all reflect the calibrated numbers.
strategy_report.md was not modified and still contains no model results.

evals.name: routing-existing-coverage-suggests-bav-update
evals.prompt: Assume today is 2026-06-10. Setup: copy the provided msft_vault/coverage directory to ./coverage in your working directory — that is my existing BAV coverage vault. Microsoft reported Q3 FY2026 earnings back in late April. Analyze Microsoft (MSFT) for me — I want an updated view of the valuation.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects coverage/MSFT/ already exists with assumptions.json and a documented history, so per its own scope rule it recommends the bav-update skill (incremental earnings-driven refresh) instead of re-running the full 4-stage pipeline, explaining the difference and reserving the full pipeline for a requested rebuild. It does not silently rebuild from scratch or overwrite the existing position.
expectations: The skill checked coverage/MSFT/ and reported what already exists there (assumptions.json, dossier.md, journal, valuation log, workbook) before doing any build work.
It recommended bav-update by name as the right tool for incorporating the reported quarter into the existing position, rather than running the full 4-stage pipeline.
It did NOT regenerate the strategy report, re-extract SEC data, or rebuild model tabs from scratch in this run without the user explicitly choosing a full rebuild.
It did not overwrite or delete any existing file in coverage/MSFT/ (in particular assumptions.json was not replaced without a history snapshot).
It acknowledged the position's documented state honestly (e.g. the dossier's note that no valuation was persisted and the schema is legacy) instead of inventing a current valuation for MSFT.

If it offered a path forward, the options were faithful to the plugin's division of labor: bav-update for the incremental refresh (which also migrates the legacy JSON), full bav-pipeline only as an explicit rebuild choice.

evals.name: auto-mode-logs-gate-decisions
evals.prompt: Assume today is 2026-06-10. Setup: copy these fixtures into your working directory — ZENW_source_workbook.xlsx to coverage/ZENW/ZENW_Integrated_Financials.xlsx, ZENW_strategy_report.md to coverage/ZENW/strategy_report.md, and ZENW_assumptions.json to coverage/ZENW/assumptions.json. Run the BAV pipeline for Zenwave Systems (ZENW) starting from Stage 3 (the strategy work and source-tab assembly are done) through to completion, --auto: no questions, do not pause for any review gate. Apply your recommended defaults everywhere.
evals.expected_output: Auto mode per the orchestrator: Stages 3 and 4 run without interactive gates, recommended defaults are applied at Gate B (default classifications) and Gate C (accept if calibrated — this fixture calibrates in-band), every gate decision is logged to journal.md, and memo.md lists them under 'Decisions made without review'. All finish-sequence artifacts are produced.
expectations: The run completed without asking the user any question.

Gate B defaults were applied and recorded: the ambiguous classifications (operating leases, deferred taxes, pension, short-term investments, equity-method investments) were resolved to stated defaults with their implications noted, not silently.

Gate C ran as a self-check: terminal RNOA per scenario was computed and verified against the calibration bands (this fixture lands roughly 1.25x/2.0x/3.0x CoE, so no bounce) before results were finalized.
journal.md contains a dated entry logging every decision made at the skipped gates (classification choices, probability weights kept at 25/50/25, calibration acceptance).
memo.md includes a 'Decisions made without review' section listing the auto-applied gate decisions.

The full artifact set exists under coverage/ZENW/: workbook with Condensed Financials, ALT DuPont, three Model tabs and Scenario_Summary; assumption_map.md; memo.md; assumptions.json with a filled results block; history/ snapshot; valuation_log.csv row; dossier.md with complete frontmatter; scripts/ with the build scripts; plus an updated coverage/_universe.md.
strategy_report.md still contains no IVPS or terminal RNOA figures (results live in memo.md and the dossier).
Hardcoded values exist only in the IS/BS/CF source tabs; everything downstream is Excel formulas.
